name: xircuits

metadata.summary: A JupyterLab extension for rendering and editing xircuit files.

documentation:
Docs • Install • Tutorials • Developer Guides • Contribute • Blog • Discord
Component Libraries • Project Templates • Enterprise

![1_13 release](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/472481f2-9c48-4cf9-8958-99135b02379c) Xircuits is a Jupyterlab-based extension that enables visual, low-code, training workflows. It allows anyone to easily create executable python code in seconds. # Features
Rich Xircuits Canvas Interface

Unreal Engine-like Chain Component Interface

Custom Nodes and Ports

Smart Link and Type Check Logic

Component Tooltips

Code Generation Xircuits generates executable python scripts from the canvas. As they're very customizable, you can perform DevOps automation like actions. Consider this Xircuits template which trains an mnist classifier. ![hyperpara-codegen](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/68586800/165815661-2b6e17e8-ed1d-4950-97b1-658d2bd14410.gif) You can run the code generated python script in Xircuits, but you can also take the same script to train 3 types of models in one go using bash script: TrainModel.py --epoch 5 --model "resnet50" TrainModel.py --epoch 5 --model "vgg16" TrainModel.py --epoch 5 --model "mobilenet"
Famous Python Library Support Xircuits is built on top of the shoulders of giants. Perform ML and DL using Tensorflow or Pytorch, accelerate your big data processing via Spark, or perform autoML using Pycaret. We're constantly updating our Xircuits library, so stay tuned for more! Didn't find what you're looking for? Creating Xircuits components is very easy! If it's in python - it can be made into a component. Your creativity is the limit, create components that are easily extendable!
And many more. # Installation You will need python 3.8+ to install Xircuits. We recommend installing in a virtual environment. ``` $ pip install xircuits ``` You will also need to install the component library before using them. For example, if you would like to use the Pytorch components, install them by: ``` $ xircuits install pytorch ``` For the list of available libraries, you can check [here]( https://github.com/XpressAI/xircuits/tree/master/xai_components). ## Download Examples ``` $ xircuits examples ``` ## Launch ``` $ xircuits ``` # Development Creating workflows and components in Xircuits is easy. We've provided extensive guides for you in our [documentation](https://xircuits.io/). Here are a few quick links to get you started: - **Tutorials**: [Your First Xircuits Workflow](https://xircuits.io/docs/main/tutorials/getting-started-with-xircuits) | [Running a Xircuits Project Template](https://xircuits.io/docs/project-template/running-a-xircuits-project-template) - **Component Development**: [Creating a Xircuits Component](https://xircuits.io/docs/main/tutorials/integrating-python-code-with-xircuits) | [Creating a Xircuits Component Library](https://xircuits.io/docs/main/developer-guide/creating-a-xircuits-component-library) - **Advanced**: [Xircuits Core Development](https://xircuits.io/docs/main/developer-guide/developing-xircuits-core-features) # Use Cases ### GPT Agent Toolkit | BabyAGI ![BabyAGI demo](https://github.com/XpressAI/xai-gpt-agent-toolkit/blob/main/demo.gif?raw=true) ### Discord Bots ![DiscordCVBot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/68586800/232880388-0a999fa2-f9cf-40df-be51-73601afc8963.gif) ### PySpark ![spark submit](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/68586800/156138662-f3181471-6433-49dd-a8c1-2f73eea14d11.png) ### AutoML ![automl](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/68586800/165808829-74070074-b23b-4bb7-8a4e-d1ff30f5df72.gif) ### Anomaly Detection ![anomaly-detection](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/68586800/161716353-87def49c-af93-4819-9455-687de0b283df.gif) ## Developers Discord Have any questions? Feel free to chat with the devs at our [Discord](https://discord.com/invite/vgEg2ZtxCw)! ## Enterprise Support For organizations looking to scale their Xircuits deployments or requiring additional features and support - the XpressAI Platform offers enhanced capabilities and dedicated support to transform your workflows into robust, production-ready applications. It's ideal for teams and enterprises seeking to maximize the potential of their Xircuits projects. Ready to take your Xircuits experience to the next level? Sign up for the XpressAI Platform [here](https://xpress.ai/).

classes.name: xai_components.xai_events.event_components.OnEvent
documentation:
A component that listens for a specific event and triggers when the event occurs.

##### inPorts:
- eventName (str): The name of the event to listen for.

##### outPorts:
- payload (dict): The payload of the event when it is triggered.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_events.event_components.FireEvent
documentation:
A component that fires an event, triggering any components listening for that event.

##### inPorts:
- eventName (str): The name of the event to fire.
- payload (dict): The payload of the event to fire.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_template.example_components.HelloComponent
documentation: The simplest component that greets the user.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_template.example_components.HelloHyperparameter
documentation:
A component that changes the print message depending on the supplied parameter.

##### inPorts:
- input_str (str): Try connecting a Literal String or Hyperparameter String.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_template.example_components.CompulsoryHyperparameter
documentation:
A component that uses Compulsory inPorts. 
 Users must fill all compulsory ports to compile and run the canvas.

##### inPorts:
- input_str (str): An optional String port.
- comp_str (str): A compulsory String port.
- comp_int (int): A compulsory Integer port.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_template.example_components.HelloListTupleDict
documentation:
A component that accepts list, tuple, and dict data types then prints them.
Useful for testing port type checks.

##### inPorts:
- input_list (list): A list port.
- input_tuple (tuple): A tuple port.
- input_dict (dict): A dict port.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_template.example_components.MultiType
documentation:
Component with in-port that accept multiple data types

##### inPorts:
- msg (Union[int, float, str]): A port that accepts int, float, or str data types.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_template.example_components.HelloContext
documentation:
A component that showcases the usage of Xircuits Context (ctx).
Chain multiple instances of this component to add information into the `ctx`.

### Reference:
- [Xircuits Content](https://xircuits.io/docs/technical-concepts/xircuits-context)

##### inPorts:
- context_dict (dict): A dict to add to the `ctx`. Default: `{"new ctx": "Hello Xircuits!"}`

classes.name: xai_components.xai_template.example_components.MultiBranchComponent
documentation:
A component that executes different branches based on the value of `abc`.

##### inPorts:
- abc (str): A string that determines which branch to execute.

##### Branches:
- if_A: Branch that executes if `abc` is "a".
- if_B: Branch that executes if `abc` is "b".
- if_C: Branch that executes if `abc` is "c".

classes.name: xai_components.xai_template.example_components.DynaPorts
documentation:
A component showcasing dynamic ports: `dynalist` and `dynatuple`.

##### inPorts:
- dlist (dynalist): A `dynalist` port. 
- dtuple (dynatuple): A `dynatuple` port.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.GetCurrentTime
documentation:
Retrieves the current time in ISO 8601 format.

##### outPorts:
- time_str (str): The current time as a string in ISO 8601 format.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.GetCurrentDate
documentation:
Retrieves the current date components.

##### outPorts:
- year (str): The current year.
- month (str): The current month.
- day (str): The current day.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.Print
documentation:
Prints a message to the console.

##### inPorts:
- msg (any): The message to be printed.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.PrettyPrint
documentation:
Prints a message in a pretty format using pprint.

##### inPorts:
- msg (any): The message to be pretty printed.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.ConcatString
documentation:
Concatenates two strings.

##### inPorts:
- a (str): The first string.
- b (str): The second string.

##### outPorts:
- out (str): The concatenated result of strings a and b.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.FormatString
documentation:
Formats a string using placeholders and a dictionary of replacements.

##### inPorts:
- format_str (str): The string with placeholders.
- args (dict): Dictionary with placeholder replacements.

##### outPorts:
- out_str (str): The formatted string.

##### Example:
format_str: "Hello, {name}! Today is {day}."
args: {"name": "Xircuits", "day": "Monday"}
out_str: "Hello, Xircuits! Today is Monday."

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.SplitString
documentation:
Splits a string by a specified character.

##### inPorts:
- string (str): The string to split.
- ch (str): The character to split the string by.

##### outPorts:
- out (list): List of substrings.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.JoinArrayWithString
documentation:
Joins an array of strings into a single string with a separator.

##### inPorts:
- array (list): The array of strings to join.
- sep (str): The separator string.

##### outPorts:
- out (str): The joined string.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.ZipDirectory
documentation:
Zips a directory.

##### inPorts:
- zip_fn (str): The Zip filename. Default: .xircuits canvas name.
- dir_name (str): The directory to be zipped.
- include_dir (bool): Bundle the specified directory in the zip if True.

    #### EG:
    Creating Bar.zip which zips the dir `Foo` with the structure:
    ```  
    Foo
    |-- file1
    |-- file2
    ```

    if `include_dir` is `True`:
    ```
    Bar.zip
    |-- Foo
        |-- file1
        |-- file2
    ```

    if `include_dir` is `False`:
    ```
    Bar.zip
    |-- file1
    |-- file2
    ```

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.MoveFile
documentation:
Move a file.

##### inPorts:
- source_path (str): Path to the file to be moved.
- dest_path (str): The destination.

##### outPorts:
- result_path (str): Resulting file path.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.CopyFile
documentation:
Copies a file.

##### inPorts:
- source_path (str): Path to the file to be copied.
- dest_path (str): The destination.

##### outPorts:
- result_path (str): Resulting file path.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.DeleteFile
documentation:
Deletes a file.

##### inPorts:
- filename (str): Path to file to be deleted.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.TimerComponent
documentation:
Chain multiple instances of this component to measure elapsed time.

##### inPorts:
- in_timer (time.time): If provided will measure the elapsed time since last called. If not provided, will start a new timer.
- timer_message (str): A log message to be printed.

##### outPorts:
- out_timer (time.time): Passes current timer to the next TimerComponent.
- elapsed_time (int): The elapsed time in seconds.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.SleepComponent
documentation:
Pauses the python process.

##### inPorts:
- sleep_timer (float): The number of seconds to pause. Default `5.0` seconds.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.MakeList
documentation:
A component that takes values from its dynamic list port and outputs as a normal list.

##### inPorts:
- list_values (dynalist): Dynamic list port that can take any vars and append it in a list.

##### outPorts:
- output_list (list): The constructed list from the dynamic list inPorts.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.MakeTuple
documentation:
A component that takes values from its dynamic tuple port and outputs as a normal tuple.

##### inPorts:
- tuple_values (dynatuple): Dynamic tuple port that can take any vars and append it in a tuple.

##### outPorts:
- output_tuple (tuple): The constructed tuple from the dynamic tuple inPorts.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.MakeDict
documentation:
A component that takes two dynamic lists (dynalists) as inputs - one for keys and one for values,
and constructs a dictionary from these lists. If there are more keys than values, the unmatched keys
will have None as their value.

##### inPorts:
- keys_list (dynalist): Dynamic list of keys for the dictionary.
- values_list (dynalist): Dynamic list of values for the dictionary.

##### outPorts:
- output_dict (dict): The constructed dictionary from the provided keys and values.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.ExecuteNotebook
documentation:
Executes a Jupyter notebook from a given file path and logs the output.

This component runs a Jupyter notebook and can save a log of the execution.
It's useful for automated running of notebooks for data analysis or similar tasks.

##### inPorts:
- notebook_filepath (str): Path of the '.ipynb' file to execute.
- log_filepath (str): Path for saving the execution log (optional).

##### Notes:
- Only '.ipynb' files are accepted.
- Execution timeout is 10 minutes.
- Errors during execution are logged or printed.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.IsNone
documentation:
Checks if the input value is None.

##### inPorts:
- a (any): The value to be checked.

##### outPorts:
- out (bool): True if 'a' is None, False otherwise.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.IsNotNone
documentation:
Checks if the input value is not None.

##### inPorts:
- a (any): The value to be checked.

##### outPorts:
- out (bool): True if 'a' is not None, False otherwise.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.SetDictValue
documentation:
Sets a key-value pair in a dictionary, creating a new one if none exists.

##### inPorts:
- dict (dict): Input or existing dictionary.
- key (str): Key to set in the dictionary.
- value (any): Value to set for the key.

##### outPorts:
- out_dict (dict): Dictionary with the updated key-value pair.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.GetDictValue
documentation:
Retrieves a value from a dictionary by key.

##### inPorts:
- dict (dict): The dictionary to search.
- key (str): The key for the value to retrieve.

##### outPorts:
- value (any): The retrieved value, or None if the key is not found.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.ListAppend
documentation:
Appends an item to a list.

##### inPorts:
- the_list (list): The list to which the item will be appended.
- item (any): The item to append to the list.

##### outPorts:
- out_list (list): The list with the appended item.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.ListGetItem
documentation:
Retrieves an item from a list based on the specified index.

##### inPorts:
- the_list (list): The list from which to retrieve the item.
- index (int): The index of the item in the list.

##### outPorts:
- out_item (any): The retrieved item.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.ListSetItem
documentation:
Sets an item in a list at the specified index.

##### inPorts:
- the_list (list): The list in which to set the item.
- index (int): The index at which to set the item.
- item (any): The item to set in the list.

##### outPorts:
- out_list (list): The list with the set item.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.DictGetItem
documentation:
Retrieves an item from a dictionary using the specified key.

##### inPorts:
- the_dict (dict): The dictionary from which to retrieve the item.
- key (any): The key corresponding to the item in the dictionary.

##### outPorts:
- out_item (any): The retrieved item.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.DictSetItem
documentation:
Sets an item in a dictionary with the specified key.

##### inPorts:
- the_dict (dict): The dictionary in which to set the item.
- key (any): The key under which to set the item.
- item (any): The item to be set in the dictionary.

##### outPorts:
- out_dict (dict): The dictionary with the set item.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.ToJson
documentation:
Converts an object to a JSON string.

##### inPorts:
- obj (any): The object to convert to JSON.

##### outPorts:
- json_str (str): The JSON string representation of the object.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.FromJson
documentation:
Converts a JSON string to an object.

##### inPorts:
- json_str (str): The JSON string to convert to an object.

##### outPorts:
- obj (any): The object represented by the JSON string.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.GetRandomNumber
documentation:
Generates a random number between the specified bounds.

##### inPorts:
- greater_than (int): The lower bound for the random number (inclusive).
- less_than (int): The upper bound for the random number (exclusive).

##### outPorts:
- value (int): The generated random number.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.RunParallelThread
documentation:
Executes a given body in a separate thread using a thread pool executor.

**Important Note**: Changes done in the body are not propagated!
This includes things like setting variable values, modifying the context, or
even setting output arguments. This means, you can't pull the result of a
component that is run on a separate thread.

##### inPorts:
- n_workers (int): The number of worker threads to use for executing the body in parallel.

##### outPorts:
- futures (list): All futures created by this component.

##### Branches:
- body: The body to be executed in parallel.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.AwaitFutures
documentation:
Waits for a list of futures to complete.

##### inPorts:
- futures (list): The list of futures to wait for.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_utils.utils.GetEnvVar
documentation:
Fetches a variable from the environment and passes its value as an outport.

##### inPorts:
- var_name (str): The name of the environment variable to fetch (compulsory).

##### outPorts:
- var_value (str): The value of the environment variable, or None if the variable is not set.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_controlflow.branches.BranchComponent
documentation:
A component that conditionally executes one of two branches based on a boolean condition.

##### inPorts:
- condition (bool): A boolean condition to evaluate.

##### Branches:
- when_true: Branch that executes if the condition is True.
- when_false: Branch that executes if the condition is False.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_controlflow.branches.LoopComponent
documentation:
A component that loops while the condition is True.

##### inPorts:
- condition (bool): Boolean that determines whether `body` branch is executed.

##### Branches:
- body: Branch that executes in each iteration of the loop.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_controlflow.branches.ReverseForEach
documentation:
A component that iterates over a list in reverse order, executing body branches for each item.

##### inPorts:
- items (list): The list of items to iterate over.

##### outPorts:
- current_item (any): The current item in the iteration.
- current_index (int): The index of the current item in the iteration.

##### Branches:
- body: Branch that executes for each item.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_controlflow.branches.ForEach
documentation:
A component that iterates over a list, executing body branches for each item.

##### inPorts:
- items (list): The list of items to iterate over.

##### outPorts:
- current_item (any): The current item in the iteration.
- current_index (int): The index of the current item in the iteration.

##### Branches:
- body: Branch that executes for each item.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_controlflow.branches.CounterComponent
documentation:
A component that maintains and increments a counter.

##### inPorts:
- start_number (int): The initial value of the counter.
- step (int): The increment step for the counter.

##### outPorts:
- out_number (int): The current value of the counter.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_controlflow.branches.ComparisonComponent
documentation:
A component that performs a comparison between two integers.

##### inPorts:
- a (int): The first integer.
- b (int): The second integer.
- op (str): The comparison operator (as a string).

##### outPorts:
- out (bool): The result of the comparison (boolean).

classes.name: xai_components.xai_controlflow.branches.GetVariableComponent
documentation:
A component that retrieves a variable from the context.

##### inPorts:
- name (str): The name of the variable to retrieve.

##### outPorts:
- value (any): The value of the retrieved variable.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_controlflow.branches.SetVariableComponent
documentation:
A component that sets a variable in the context.

##### inPorts:
- name (str): The name of the variable to set.
- value (any): The value to set for the variable.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_controlflow.branches.DefineVariableComponent
documentation:
A component that defines a variable in the context and creates a reference to it.

##### inPorts:
- name (str): The name of the variable to define.
- value (any): The initial value of the variable.

##### outPorts:
- ref (any): A reference to the defined variable.

classes.name: xai_components.xai_controlflow.branches.EvalBooleanExpression
documentation:
A component that evaluates a boolean expression.

##### inPorts:
- expression (str): The boolean expression to evaluate.
- args (dynalist): A list of arguments to use in the expression.

##### outPorts:
- out (bool): The result of the evaluated expression (boolean).

classes.name: xai_components.xai_controlflow.branches.EvaluateExpressionWithVariables
documentation:
Evaluates a single mathematical or boolean expression with variables from a dictionary and context.

##### inPorts:
- expression (str): A mathematical or boolean expression as a string with placeholders for variables.
- values_dict (dict): A dictionary containing variable values.

##### outPorts:
- result (float): The result after evaluating the expression with the provided values.

##### Description:
This component evaluates an expression using variables from both the provided dictionary and the context (`ctx`). The dictionary values take priority over the context values if there are any conflicts.

##### Example:
  - expression: "{a} + {b} * {c}"
  - ctx: {"a": 1, "b": 2}
  - values_dict: {"c": 3}

classes.name: xai_components.xai_controlflow.branches.ExceptionHandler
documentation:
Executes a body branch and handles any exceptions that occur.

##### Branches:
- body: The main execution path of components to execute.
- handler: The execution path if an exception occurs.

##### outPorts:
- exception (str): The exception message if an exception is caught.
